objective: Compare active-cooling architectures for a storage-transmon-readout cQED device and determine which scheme is fastest, most robust to transmon decoherence, and best overall on the local cqed_sim device abstraction.
completed_tasks: Implemented the comparative study runner and exported machine-readable artifacts.
Selected the best pulsed, resonant continuous, and detuned continuous settings on the local device model.
Ran initial-state comparisons, robustness sweeps, readout-linewidth sweeps, and representative convergence checks.
Generated figures, report source, reproducibility notebook, and handoff documents.

Iteration 2: Rebuilt report with literature-grounded Introduction, System and Methods, quantitative convergence table, sensitivity/uncertainty analysis, fixed summary figure, integrated all evidence figures, expanded reproducibility appendix.
Iteration 3: Reconciled the canonical metric definitions across scheme summaries, study results, and report tables.
Iteration 3: Regenerated the scheme summary, convergence, and initial-state artifacts with matched pulsed ladder depth and aligned baseline time-step choices.
Iteration 3: Rewrote the contradictory report claims so the pulsed e-fold time, full protocol duration, and time-step caveats are all stated consistently.
pending_tasks: Re-run and verify the reproducibility notebook against the refreshed artifacts.
Decide whether to extend the continuous-scheme convergence study further or submit the current caveated report for re-review.

key_results.recommended_first_experiment: Pulsed ladder with shorter ringdown than the conservative 4/κ choice.
key_results.best_long_term_direction: Detuned Raman-like readout-assisted cooling with a stronger readout bath.

review_history.summary: Saved results appear internally consistent, but the report lacks literature context, a methods section, quantitative convergence and uncertainty presentation, a working summary figure, and a complete reproducibility appendix.

Iteration 2 fixed the report structure, methods, and appendix issues from the first review, but the revised manuscript remains internally inconsistent: headline single-photon, higher-Fock, convergence, and artifact values are not quantitatively reconciled, so the core evidence-to-claim chain is still unreliable.
